threshold_count: Complaint of sleep quality/amount (initial, interval, or terminal insomnia)

symptoms.description: The patient has persistent difficulty initiating or falling asleep.
The patient has difficulty maintaining sleep, frequently awakening during the night.
The patient awakens too early in the morning and is unable to return to sleep.
The patient frequently wakes up feeling that their sleep was unrestful or unrefreshing.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Severe daytime drowsiness despite 7+ hours of sleep (e.g., naps, sleep inertia)

symptoms.description: The patient takes repeated daytime naps or unintentionally falls asleep during the day despite having had sufficient nighttime sleep.
The patient experiences prolonged sleep episodes of nine or more hours that remain unrefreshing.
The patient experiences sleep inertia, feeling groggy, disoriented, or having difficulty remaining fully awake upon abrupt awakening.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Irresistible attacks of waketime sleep associated with cataplexy or physiological markers (hypocretin deficiency)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences irrepressible attacks of sleep or an overwhelming need to sleep during the daytime.
The patient experiences sudden, brief episodes of muscle weakness or paralysis, known as cataplexy, which are often triggered by strong emotions.
The patient experiences vivid, often frightening, visual or auditory hallucinations when falling asleep or waking up.
The patient is temporarily unable to move or speak while transitioning between sleep and wakefulness.

threshold_count: 5+ apneas/hypopneas per hour + symptoms (snoring, drowsiness) OR 15+ episodes/hour regardless of symptoms

symptoms.description: The patient experiences frequent pauses in breathing, such as apneas or hypopneas, during sleep due to airway obstruction.
The patient exhibits loud snoring, snorting, or gasping during sleep.
The patient experiences daytime sleepiness, fatigue, or unrefreshing sleep resulting from nighttime breathing disturbances.

threshold_count: Polysomnography shows 5+ central sleep apneas per hour of sleep

symptoms.description: The patient experiences frequent central sleep apneas during sleep, characterized by a cessation of breathing effort.

threshold_count: Polysomnography shows periods of reduced breathing with elevated CO2 levels

symptoms.description: The patient experiences episodes of shallow breathing or hypoventilation during sleep that lead to abnormal blood gas levels.
The patient demonstrates periods of reduced breathing accompanied by elevated carbon dioxide levels during polysomnography.

threshold_count: Mismatch between natural body rhythms and environmental/work demands (e.g., Shift Work, Delayed Phase)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a recurring mismatch between their biological sleep-wake cycle and the environmental or social demands placed upon them.
The patient experiences notable insomnia or hypersomnolence as a result of a circadian rhythm misalignment.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Non-Rapid Eye Movement Sleep Arousal Disorder
threshold_count: Repeated incomplete awakenings featuring sleepwalking or sleep terrors
duration_rule: Recurrent; typically in first third of the night

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits episodes of rising from bed during sleep and walking about with a blank, staring expression, often remaining unresponsive to others.
The patient abruptly awakens from sleep with a terrified scream, accompanied by intense fear and signs of autonomic arousal.
The patient tends to have little or no memory of the sleep arousal episode the following morning.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Repeated instant awakenings from long, terrifying dreams with detailed recall
duration_rule: Recurrent; typically in second half of the night

symptoms.description: The patient instantly and fully awakens from terrifying dreams and recalls them in frightening detail.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Rapid Eye Movement Sleep Behavior Disorder
threshold_count: Complex physical actions/vocalizations during REM sleep (acting out dreams)

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits vocalizations or complex motor behaviors during REM sleep, often acting out vivid dreams.
The patient lacks the normal muscle atonia that typically accompanies REM sleep, as revealed by polysomnography.

threshold_count: Irresistible urge to move legs due to unpleasant sensations, worse during rest/evenings

symptoms.description: The patient experiences an irresistible urge to move the legs, usually accompanied by uncomfortable or unpleasant sensations in the lower limbs.
The patient experiences an urge to move the legs that begins or worsens during periods of rest or inactivity.
The patient experiences an urge to move the legs that worsens in the evening or at night.
The patient experiences an urge to move that is partially or totally relieved by movement, such as walking or stretching.

threshold_count: Prominent and severe sleep disturbance caused by a substance
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient complains of a severe sleep disturbance, such as insomnia or hypersomnolence, directly resulting from substance or medication use.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
